content:
There are 3 characters and their wheels which stand out. The Two Hypercarries **Tawil** and **Helot: Catena**, and the S Tier Support **Thais**

**Tawil - Wheel Unseen**  
If you don't have her, Tawil will be the number one priority. Her playstyle is of "cantriping", play cards that give you resources/buffs/more cards. If you enjoy drawing cards, tutuoring and comboing out she is great for you.

She is a "Hypercarry" in the sense that she circumvents game mechanics. You know how if you "kill" and enemy only for it to rouse (revive) and become damage immune? Tawil can literally kill an enemy through damage immunity.

This is gamebreaking because damage immunity is supposed to be a speedbump. Its supposed to make you waste all your stacks of temporary buffs and start over from zero. Tawil allows you to continue to deal damage at your strongest state.

In addition her Wheel (Wheel Unseen) is ridiculously good. It gives +2 hand limit. Hand limit is by far the best stat on wheels. No only is hand limit useful, but its effectively a "passive, team wide buff" so it will always have a place in any team, even at E0.

Suggested Level: E0/E2/OE its all good.
Ryker is also a great support for her, and is currently rate up (E0 or E1 him)

**Helot: Catena - Drowning in Crimson**  
Helot: Catena (or GHelot) is another extremely strong hypercarry. Her playstyle is more of "burst". You accumulate a bunch of buffs / resources, and then you spend it all on one super powerful turn.

Just like Tawil she also circumvents game mechanics via the sheer amount of bleed she can inflict. This allows her to get around damage immunity, as well as "Concealment Tide".

The only downside is that her wheel is not as good in general. Her wheel is also amazing, providing Hand Limit and a lot of damage and card draw.

Suggested Level:E0/E3/OE any level is great

UID: 100566618

**Thais - Merciful Nurturing**
After reading all of that you are probably prepared to dust off your hypercarry and crush some event stage thats way above your level right?

Incorrect. In fact it’s way better to use someone else’s Tawil or GHelot. You can find a whale and borrow his Maxed out +12 GHelot with +12 Gacha Wheels. Just go on rankings (I recommend the bottom tab of your friendlist, the Alumni Keep Level Leaderboard) and just start friending everyone there. Soon a lot of them will friend you back.

Then you can assemble a team like Thais, Horla (or literally any other Ulta unit to get Ulta space), Aigis (Free unit), and your friendly neighborhood’s whale GHelot. And you can beat event madness difficulty and weeklies even with a very underleveled team and maybe an emergency gnosis.

Thais is an extremely strong support and works very well supporting GHelot. In addition her wheel is one of the best in the game and provides unconditional passive hand limit.

Suggested Level: E0/E2/E3 any level is great.
Will add to this that the banner is very good to take the unit and the wheel, but if you want to get specific dupes after, it is more of a gamble.
